q: Which design thinking component allows you to put your assumptions aside and understand the user's problems, pain points, and aspirations?
explanation: Empathy is crucial to any human-centered design process as it allows us to set aside our own assumptions and gain insight into users and their needs.

q: What is a leading practice for designing clear and effective user interfaces?
options: Use a varied color palette to create visual variety
Keep layouts consistent and focus on essential elements only
Avoid providing feedback to keep the interface uncluttered
Ensure interfaces are sparse and contain lots of white space
correct: Keep layouts consistent and focus on essential elements only
explanation: Clear and effective interfaces provide a consistent experience that does not overwhelm users with unnecessary options.

q: What is a key benefit of using UI Builder?
options: It automatically generates scripts for all use cases
It is only accessible via App Engine Studio to developers
It allows drag-and-drop creation of pages using components
It does not require any understanding of web components
correct: It allows drag-and-drop creation of pages using components
explanation: UI Builder supports drag-and-drop development of component-based pages.

q: When using a card component, what is a leading practice for displaying content?
options: Display as much detail as possible for transparency
Use multiple highlighted values per card for emphasis
explanation: Cards should be minimal and easy to scan to support user decisions.

q: Which of the following enhances form usability for diverse user audiences? Select all that apply.
options: Using technical field names visible to users
Logical groupings of fields in sections
correct: Logical groupings of fields in sections
explanation: Designing for usability involves clear, accessible, and well-structured interfaces. Complex dropdowns and exposing technical field names negatively affect the experience.

q: What are the components that can help you create a successful content strategy?
explanation: Content-oriented and people-oriented components can help you create a successful content strategy.

q: Which of the following organization schemes categorizes information in a way that is specific to or defined by the organization or industry?
explanation: Subjective organization schemes categorize information in a way specific to the organization or industry. Though difficult to design, they are more useful than exact schemes.

q: What are the drawbacks of deep structures? Select all that apply.
options: Users may have to click through too many levels to find information
Important content can become buried and harder to discover
It results in inconsistent operational and maintenance costs
It may require long and confusing menus
Users may become frustrated or abandon the task
They make it harder to apply alphabetical sorting
correct: Users may have to click through too many levels to find information
Important content can become buried and harder to discover
Users may become frustrated or abandon the task
explanation: Deep structures bury information beneath too many layers, burden users to navigate through several levels, and as a result users may abandon the task.

q: What can users access from a portal page?
explanation: A Service Portal is a collection of pages offering users an engaging way to access applications, information, services, and tasks.

q: Your customer wants the user to complete information using checkboxes to start their search. What search pattern should they use?
explanation: The parametric search pattern asks users to make selections (checkboxes, dropdowns) to start their search. Can return very few or no results depending on the combination of parameters.

q: Which of the following are true about widgets? Select all that apply.
options: Widgets execute a specified behavior on a page
The same widget can be added once per page
A widget is technically an AngularJS Directive
correct: Widgets execute a specified behavior on a page
A widget is technically an AngularJS Directive
explanation: Widgets execute behavior, display content, perform one function, and are AngularJS directives. The same widget can be added multiple times per page.

q: Which of the following provides a list of all widgets included on all Service Portal pages?
explanation: The Portal Analyzer provides a list of all widgets included on all Service Portal pages.

q: What are the three phases of Design Thinking?
a: Discover: understand the problem through empathy and user observation
Ideate: generate ideas and validate with users to narrow to the best solution
Deliver: rapid iteration — prototype, test, refine, launch

q: What is Miller's Law and why does it matter for UX design?
a: People can hold 5-9 items in short-term memory. Chunk information into groups of 7 (±2) to support learnability and reduce cognitive load.

q: What is Tesler's Law (Conservation of Complexity)?
a: Remove as much complexity as possible from the user and build it into the system — but things can only be simplified to a certain point before they stop functioning.

q: What is Poka-Yoke in UX design?
a: Mistake-proofing: place constraints that force users to correct their behavior before they can proceed with an action.

q: What is the difference between an affordance and a signifier in interaction design?
a: An affordance is a visual clue (color, shape, size) suggesting how an element works. A signifier provides explicit information about what will happen before the user acts — e.g. a button label or instructions before form submission.

q: What is the default portal URL suffix in the ServiceNow base system?
a: /esc (Employee Center)

q: What do the four widget colors in the Widget Diagnostic Tool indicate?
a: Green: base system widget, unmodified
Yellow: cloned widget
Blue: new custom widget
Red: customized base widget (code, schema, or dependencies changed)

q: What is the difference between HTML, server scripts, and client scripts in a widget?
a: HTML: renders the view, binds client script variables, gathers user input
Server scripts: load initial data, run server-side queries, send record data to the client controller
Client scripts: map server data to client objects, process data before rendering, pass user input back to server

q: What is the difference between exact and subjective organization schemes?
a: Exact schemes (alphabetical, chronological, geographical) divide content into clear, mutually exclusive categories — easy to maintain but require users to know where something fits. Subjective schemes (topic, task, audience, metaphor) are specific to the organization or industry — harder to design but more useful to users.

q: What are the three types of navigation systems and when is each used?
a: Structural: follows site hierarchy, enables movement to pages above or below current page
Associative: links related content regardless of location — often embedded in text, easy to miss
Utility: site tools outside the main hierarchy — site map, help, login/logout, font size

q: What are the key limitations of the Widget Diagnostic Tool?
a: Cannot fix or revert code directly — must navigate to the widget record
Navigating to another portal page disables the tool
Only shows first-level widget dependencies
Color-based categorization is not accessible for visually impaired users
Cannot track deleted widget dependencies

q: What data does the Portal Analyzer store, and where?
a: Stored in sp_portal_analyzer. Fields: Page, Page ID, Widget, Widget ID, Status (OOTB/Cloned/Customized/New), Page views, User count. Running the job deletes and replaces all existing records.

q: Why should you use GlideAJAX instead of synchronous server calls in client scripts?
a: GlideAJAX makes fast, dynamic, asynchronous calls to the server without blocking the UI. Synchronous calls freeze the browser while waiting for a response, degrading the user experience.

q: Why is Universal Request valuable for Employee Experience (ESM)?
a: It provides a single entry point for all service requests across IT, HR, Facilities, and Legal. The system routes automatically and handles cross-department handovers invisibly — employees never need to re-explain their issue or guess which department to contact.
